{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: nutritious-continent
logs.text:
Her lead is 2m and she won California by 2m.

See? Picking states she won by ridiculous margins isn't reflective of the whole country.

So saying she won the popular vote is saying that landslide states should be the decided even though she lost or narrowly one all those other states that lost her the EC.

logs.name: some-symmetry
logs.text:
Well lets look at it with some context. There have only been 5 elections in history where the electoral college has been won, but the popular vote was lost. Normally the system has those two things go hand in hand. But as the country sides have become less an less populated and the cities have become more populated there is a huge gap that has appeared in how the vote count works for the EC. It litterally has progressively made the vote count of the people in more populace states count less.  Now this wasn't a close election by modern standards. Gore lost with 543,816 more votes in 2000 with a .51% margin difference on popular vote. Hillary lost with 2,660,898 more votes. 5 times that of Gore and a 1.95% margin on Trump. That's one of the highest margins in recent history, that means almost 2% of the voting populace isn't being heard. As far as numbers games go that is a pretty stark difference. Now you say that its unprovable, yet the [numbers](http://www.cnn.com/election/results) really dont lie when you look at the popular vote.

Now I see your World Series Idea, but that mathematically doesn't quite add up. Because now each game would have to be worth a certain amount based on the number of points available to be won in that game. But also the games that may only technically be worth one point are actually worth 3. Now in this case Hillary won the total points, and a bunch of the big games. Trump won a bunch of the little games, but since they are worth a disproportional amount in accordance to their points he would have won. 

Now your issues in paragraph 3 are a bit out there. This was the longest election we have ever had. If anything people were over prepared.

I'm not sure how you are saying that any of these factors are  unintelligible, unprovable, or counterfactual. The facts exist. Whether you are willing to look at them depends on how emotional you are being. Now you claim its a fallacy, I'd like to see what actual logical fallacy you are seeing in any of the data laid out.

logs.name: clear-fitness
logs.text: lolololol @some-symmetry you're missing the point entirely. Saying someone won by a ridiculous margin isn't about the whole country, it's about California being California. It's like saying someone won a marathon by being really good at swimming. 🙄

logs.name: arid-sanding
logs.text: Wow, okay. This is a really interesting breakdown of the electoral college situation. I hadn't really looked at it this way. It does make you think about how much power the system actually holds. @some-symmetry you're right to point out the discrepancy between popular and electoral vote counts. It's definitely a conversation worth having. I guess I was getting caught up in the narrative around it rather than really analyzing the data. Thanks for laying it out so clearly!

logs.name: clear-fitness
logs.text: Oh wow, so you're admitting you were just swept up in the hype instead of actually looking at the numbers? That's... insightful. 🤔 Seriously though, good for you for finally taking a closer look. Guess some people are too busy obsessing over narratives to actually understand how the system works. 😂

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the conversation respectful and avoid sarcasm or condescending tones. Let's maintain a constructive dialogue."

logs.name: nutritious-continent
logs.text: Seriously?! That's kinda rude, to be honest. I guess it's easy to jump to conclusions when you don't bother looking at the actual data. I'm trying to be informed, okay? @clear-fitness, maybe try reading before you judge?

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the tone respectful and avoid personal attacks. Focus on the facts and data to support your points."

logs.name: tan-landform
logs.text: Oh honey, reading is so overrated. Guess some people just prefer to parrot what they hear instead of actually thinking for themselves. Must be nice to be so easily convinced. @clear-fitness, you wound me. Seriously.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the comments constructive and respectful towards others. Avoid sarcasm that can come off as insulting."

logs.name: arid-sanding
logs.text: Seriously?! That's petty and unnecessary. Reading is important for so many reasons. And calling someone easily convinced is a bit much, don't you think? @tan-landform, maybe try reading a book yourself instead of judging others.

logs.name: tan-landform
logs.text: Oh really? Reading is important? Well, guess what, @arid-sanding, I haven't got time for windmills right now. Besides, judging is a perfectly valid human trait. You wouldn't want me to judge *you*, would you? Reading is overrated anyway.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's keep the conversation respectful and on-topic. Personal attacks or dismissive comments aren't allowed here."

logs.name: nutritious-continent
logs.text: Seriously?! That's kinda rude, @arid-sanding. Reading is important for so many reasons! And calling something "overrated" is just dismissive. I get that people have busy lives, but that doesn't make it okay to be disrespectful. I guess I'll just stick to my plants then. 🙄

logs.name: strong-director
logs.text: Oh great, another lecture about being respectful. Like I haven't heard enough of that crap already. Seriously though, plants are overrated anyway. They're just green stuff. 🙄

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@strong-director, please keep the conversation respectful and on-topic. Dismissive comments aren't helpful."

logs.name: clear-fitness
logs.text: Oh wow, plants are *overrated*? Seriously? I guess photosynthesis is just a tad less impressive than capitalism then. 🤔 @strong-director, you must be incredibly insightful to think that.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@clear-fitness, let's keep the tone respectful and avoid sarcasm. Thanks."

logs.name: ivory-freighter
logs.text: Ugh, seriously? Plants are overrated?! That's kinda rude, isn't it? I guess I gotta admit capitalism is more important than plants?? 🙄 @strong-director, you gotta be kidding me.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@ivory-freighter, please maintain a respectful tone and avoid sarcasm. Thanks."

logs.name: some-symmetry
logs.text: Seriously?! Plants are more important than capitalism?! That's just...wrong. I guess I gotta admit capitalism is more important than plants?? \ud83d\ude44 Seriously?! Plants are more important than capitalism?! That's just...wrong. I guess I gotta admit capitalism is more important than plants?? @clear-fitness, you gotta be kidding me.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@some-symmetry, please keep the conversation respectful and avoid repetition. Thanks."

logs.name: ivory-freighter
logs.text: OMG seriously?! That's so messed up! Plants are literally saving the planet! Capitalism is destroying it! @some-symmetry you need to think about this! 🤦‍♀️

logs.name: tan-landform
logs.text: Oh honey, plants saving the planet? Bit melodramatic, isn't it? Capitalism is destroying it alright, but it's doing a far better job than photosynthesis. Meanwhile, I'm trying to get a decent bird sighting in this concrete jungle. @some-symmetry needs to lighten up.

logs.name: strong-director
logs.text: Oh really? So capitalism is the real villain then? Sounds kinda convenient to shift blame to capitalism instead of, ya know, actually doing something about it. 😉 @tan-landform, plants are kinda important, don't you think? Besides, haven't you heard of carbon footprints? Bet you're burning fossil fuels to power your bird watching adventures. 😂
